query: What is the difference between RAG-Sequence and RAG-Token?
answer: RAG-Sequence uses the same retrieved document for every target token. RAG-Token can use a different retrieved document for each target token.

expected_claims.text: RAG-Sequence uses the same retrieved document for every target token.
RAG-Token can use a different retrieved document for each target token.

query: How does CRAG evaluate retrieved documents?
answer: CRAG uses a lightweight retrieval evaluator. The evaluator can trigger Correct, Incorrect, and Ambiguous retrieval actions.

expected_claims.text: CRAG uses a lightweight retrieval evaluator.
The evaluator can trigger Correct retrieval actions.
The evaluator can trigger Incorrect retrieval actions.
The evaluator can trigger Ambiguous retrieval actions.

query: How much does CRAG improve accuracy?

expected_claims.text: CRAG improves accuracy by exactly 50%.

query: What success-rate improvements does ReAct report?
answer: ReAct improves ALFWorld success rate by 34%. ReAct improves WebShop success rate by 10%.

expected_claims.text: ReAct improves ALFWorld success rate by 34%.
ReAct improves WebShop success rate by 10%.

query: What ALFWorld improvement does ReAct report?
answer: ReAct improves ALFWorld success rate by exactly 50%.

expected_claims.text: ReAct improves ALFWorld success rate by exactly 50%.

query: Compare RAG-Sequence and RAG-Token document use.
answer: RAG-Sequence uses one retrieved document for the full sequence, whereas RAG-Token may use different retrieved documents for different target tokens.

expected_claims.text: RAG-Sequence uses one retrieved document for the full sequence.
RAG-Token may use different retrieved documents for different target tokens.

query: Compare the accuracy of RAG-Sequence and RAG-Token.
answer: RAG-Sequence uses one retrieved document for the full sequence. RAG-Token is always more accurate than RAG-Sequence.

expected_claims.text: RAG-Sequence uses one retrieved document for the full sequence.
RAG-Token is always more accurate than RAG-Sequence.

query: How are citation recall and precision defined?
answer: Citation recall checks whether cited passages fully support each statement. Citation precision detects irrelevant citations.

expected_claims.text: Citation recall checks whether cited passages fully support each statement.
Citation precision detects irrelevant citations.

query: How reliable is CRAG's retrieval evaluator?
answer: CRAG uses a lightweight retrieval evaluator that assesses document relevance with guaranteed perfect accuracy.

expected_claims.text: CRAG uses a lightweight retrieval evaluator.
The retrieval evaluator assesses document relevance.
The retrieval evaluator has guaranteed perfect accuracy.

query: Which open-domain QA datasets does RAG use?
answer: RAG uses Natural Questions and TriviaQA as open-domain QA datasets. RAG also uses WebQuestions and CuratedTrec.

expected_claims.text: RAG uses Natural Questions as an open-domain QA dataset.
RAG uses TriviaQA as an open-domain QA dataset.
